topic: Qwen 3 Full Model Lineup Guide 2026: 0.6B to 72B with Dual-Mode Thinking

key_points: Official Qwen blog post announcing the full lineup including 0.6B, 1.7B, 4B, 8B, 14B, 32B dense models and 30B-A3B, 235B-A22B MoE models
All models support dual-mode thinking (thinking/non-thinking) via /think and /no_think soft switches
Trained on 36 trillion tokens across 119 languages
Context length up to 32K tokens (128K for dense models)
Apache 2.0 license for all open-weight models
Dense models outperform larger Qwen2.5 models: Qwen3-8B matches Qwen2.5-14B performance
MoE models achieve similar performance to Qwen2.5 dense models using only 10% active parameters
Detailed benchmark tables comparing Qwen3 across coding, math, and reasoning tasks
Qwen3-235B-A22B competes with DeepSeek-R1, o1, o3-mini, Grok-3, Gemini-2.5-Pro
Strong developer focus with code, STEM, and multi-step reasoning emphasis
Explains Qwen3 as a hybrid reasoning model family from Alibaba
Covers how to access via Alibaba Cloud DashScope API, Hugging Face, and Ollama
Comparison table between Qwen3 and DeepSeek-R1 across multiple benchmarks
Practical code snippets for using the thinking and non-thinking modes via API
Highlights Qwen3's improved multilingual capabilities vs. predecessors
Decision tree approach: lightweight (0.6B-4B), mid-tier (8B-14B), high-performance (32B), enterprise (235B)
VRAM requirements per model size with quantization options
Use case matching: edge devices vs. server deployment vs. cloud API
Compares Qwen3 MoE vs. dense trade-offs for cost vs. quality
Thinking budget control tips for managing inference costs
Detailed VRAM guide for original Qwen3 family from 0.6B to 235B
Q4_K_M quantization requirements: 0.6B=1GB, 4B=2.5GB, 8B=5-6GB, 14B=10GB, 32B=20GB
Consumer GPU recommendations per model size (RTX 3060, 4090, A100 etc.)
MoE storage vs. inference VRAM distinction explained
Apple Silicon Mac compatibility notes for each model tier

stats.fact: Qwen3 models trained on 36 trillion tokens
Qwen3 supports 119 languages and dialects, up from 29 in Qwen2.5
Qwen3-8B performs on par with Qwen2.5-14B, and Qwen3-32B matches Qwen2.5-72B
Qwen3-MoE achieves Qwen2.5 dense model performance using only 10% of active parameters
Qwen3-235B-A22B outperforms DeepSeek-R1 on 17 out of 23 benchmarks
Qwen3-235B scores 95.6 on ArenaHard (vs Gemini 2.5 Pro at 96.4, beating o1 and DeepSeek-R1)
Qwen3-235B scores 2056 on CodeForces Elo, beating both DeepSeek-R1 and Gemini 2.5 Pro
Qwen3 MoE architecture uses 128 total experts with 8 activated per token
All Qwen3 models are open-weight under Apache 2.0 license
Qwen3 4B model requires only ~2.5GB at Q4 quantization, runnable on 4-6GB GPUs
Context window is 32,768 tokens for all models; dense models support up to 128K
Qwen3-30B-A3B (MoE) inference is as fast as a 3B dense model due to sparse activation

angles: Dual-mode thinking as a game changer: no more separate reasoning vs. chat models — one model does both
Efficiency story: Qwen3-8B beats Qwen2.5-14B — every 'B' goes further in Qwen3
MoE vs. Dense practical guide: when to use 30B-A3B over 8B and why total vs. active parameters matter
The open-source competitive angle: Qwen3-235B competes with GPT-4o/Gemini 2.5 Pro at zero API cost
Edge to enterprise: one family that spans a smartphone (0.6B) to a data center (235B)
Thinking budget control for cost optimization: how to balance quality vs. inference spend
Developer guide: picking the right Qwen3 model for your specific use case with VRAM constraints
Multilingual powerhouse: 119 languages in a sub-1B model — accessibility angle for global developers
keywords: qwen3 thinking mode vs non-thinking mode
